paper_title: Structural and sedimentological analysis of the Pliocene–Late Miocene deposits from the Romanian Black Sea offshore based on borehole electrical imaging

methods.description: This method involves the use of a wireline logging program to record electrical imaging logs in a borehole. The purpose is to analyze the resistivity and conductivity variations in geological formations, which helps in identifying lithological and structural features.

steps.description: The wireline logging investigation program for the analyzed well included an electrical imaging log, which was recorded in the 8.5-inch borehole section over the depth interval 649-1272 m (624-1247 m TVDSS).
steps.searchable_summary: record electrical imaging log in borehole

steps.description: The electrical imaging tool used is a four-arm, pad-based microresistivity device. Measurements are made by causing a current to flow from each electrode button through the formations.
steps.searchable_summary: use microresistivity device to measure formation resistivity

steps.description: The corrections that have been applied to the raw imaging data included: filling data gaps, accelerometer correction, depth alignment of microresistivity traces, gain correction and dead/faulty buttons corrections.
steps.searchable_summary: apply corrections to raw imaging data

steps.description: Static and dynamic borehole images were created from the corrected electrical data by assigning color maps to ranges of resistivity values.
steps.searchable_summary: create static and dynamic borehole images

steps.description: For visualization, the borehole images are split along true north then the cylinder is unrolled, becoming an oriented 2D view.

methods.description: This method involves using a semi-automatic sine-curve fitting technique to obtain the dips of planar features identified on borehole images. The computed dip angles and azimuth values are used for statistical analysis and domain separation.

steps.description: A semi-automatic sine-curve fitting technique was used to obtain the dips of the planar features identified on the images.
steps.searchable_summary: apply sine-curve fitting to obtain dips

steps.description: The computed tadpoles (dip angle and dip azimuth values) allowed a statistical analysis and a separation into domains of the investigated interval.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis on dip data

methods.description: This method compares core facies against features apparent on the electrical image and their associated conventional open hole well log responses to identify electrical image facies based on lithology, bioturbation intensity, and sedimentary structure.

steps.description: The comparison of a core facies against features apparent on the electrical image and their associated conventional open hole well log responses indicated that the electrical image facies can be identified based on lithology, bioturbation intensity, and sedimentary structure.
steps.searchable_summary: compare core facies with electrical image features
